clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: To improve medication adherence in adults with hypertension through evidence-based prescribing and intervention strategies.
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: When prescribing antihypertensive medication, or when managing a patient with suboptimal blood pressure control potentially related to non-adherence.
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Select a specific adherence strategy (once-daily dosing, SPC, or reminder/education tools) based on its COR and LOE.

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for adherence strategy selection
User selects between 'Once Daily', 'SPC', or 'Reminder/Education' strategies
Display the COR, LOE, and specific recommendation text for the chosen strategy
Show practical tips for implementing the chosen strategy
User confirms the strategy will be applied to the patient's plan

strategyType.validation: Must be one of: once_daily, single_pill_combination, reminder_education

validationRules: strategyType must match one of three defined strategies

rules: If strategyType is 'once_daily', retrieve COR 1, LOE B-R recommendation
If strategyType is 'single_pill_combination', retrieve COR 1, LOE B-R recommendation
If strategyType is 'reminder_education', retrieve COR 2a, LOE B-R recommendation

categories.name: Once Daily Dosing
categories.recommendation: In adults with hypertension, antihypertensive medication dosing once daily rather than multiple times daily is beneficial to improve medication adherence.

categories.name: Single Pill Combination (SPC)
categories.recommendation: In adults with hypertension, the use of a SPC to reduce pill burden rather than taking separate pills is effective to improve medication adherence.

categories.name: Reminder Aids & Educational Interventions
categories.recommendation: In adults with hypertension, use of medication reminder aids and educational or self-management interventions can be useful to improve medication adherence.

category.description: The selected strategy name (e.g., 'Once Daily Dosing')

escalationLogic.ifAnySymptomPresent: N/A - adherence strategies are not crisis interventions

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Integrate the selected adherence strategy into the patient's antihypertensive treatment plan
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Assess adherence and blood pressure response at next follow-up visit (e.g., 4-8 weeks)
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Monitor BP at subsequent visits to evaluate effectiveness of the adherence strategy
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: If BP remains uncontrolled despite good adherence, consider alternative pharmacological approaches
nextStepRouting.documentation: Document the adherence strategy recommended and provided to the patient
